title: Xylophragma xanthophyllum
intro: Xylophragma xanthophyllum, also known as X. xanthophyllum, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, belonging to the family Acanthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Xylophragma xanthophyllum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Xylophragma xanthophyllum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Xylophragma xanthophyllum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae. It is a member of the genus Xylophragma, which consists of tropical flowering plants native to Central and South America. The plant is also known as a tree or shrub, with a moderate growth rate and attractive foliage. The family Bignoniaceae is a group of flowering plants that includes over 800 species, commonly referred to as bignonias or trumpet creepers. Xylophragma xanthophyllum is a lesser-known species within this family.
